the south half Of the Colville Indian
reservation will be open to settlers
said Congressman Wesley l.. Jones
in an address this noon before the
chamber of commerce. Congress
man Jones said there were a mil
lion and a half. acres there to be
thrown open, but that the survey
ing, allotments tot Indians and APP
praisement would take some time
yet. The announcement did not
create much enthusiasm because it
has been generally understood that
the reserve would be opened much
sooner.


The portion of the congressman S
address which did call forth great
enthusiasm was with regard to the
Columbia river. Keep your eye
on Pasco," said Mr.. Jones. "The
last congress appropriated 100000
for the opening of the Columbia
river to navigation, and with what
was already appropriated there is
now nearly million dollars for the
work. River transportation is one
sixth less than rail. Columbia river
transportation will regulate your
freight rates. 1t is mode of trans
portation which cannot be taken
into any trust, now laws can change
it. II will make all freight rates low
err. One reason got into the river
and harbor commission was so
might better devote my time to this
scheme and use the influence
would gain by being on the COM
mission"
